original_hypothesis.title: USD price proposal can become unremovable and unexecutable due to lack of any expiry/cancel path
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Once a USDUpdateProposal exists, there is no time-based expiry, no admin cancellation, and no proposer cancellation. If approvals never reach APPROVE_THRESHOLD and disapprovals never reach DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD, the proposal persists indefinitely and blocks new proposals (proposeUSDPrice reverts when proposer != address(0)). A single PriceFeed operator can grief by proposing and then the operator set can simply stop interacting, freezing all future USD updates.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a practical governance liveness failure with on-chain-only actions. Even without key compromise, operational outages or disagreement can stall price updates; an attacker with any PriceFeed role can force a stuck state without needing transaction ordering.

proof_of_signal.pattern: proposeUSDPrice/approve/disapprove require proposal state but no cancel/expiry function exists; proposeUSDPrice reverts if usdUpdateProposal.proposer != address(0)

preconditions: Attacker has any one of Role.PriceFeed_1..Role.PriceFeed_5
Attacker (or group) can ensure neither threshold is reached (e.g., by non-participation / coordination failure among operators)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Proposal lifecycle lacks a termination path besides reaching thresholds
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Admin cannot intervene unless BaseBlastManager provides an unshown override; not visible here
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time price feeds are intended to be updated

operational_questions: Is there an off-contract operational process to forcibly rotate price feed roles to recover liveness?
Does configStorage rotation of LockManager dependencies affect USD governance state?
Is usdUpdateProposal intended to be short-lived, and if so why is there no expiry?
validation_requirements: Confirm there is no external function in inheritance that deletes usdUpdateProposal
Simulate a proposal and verify new proposals are blocked indefinitely without thresholds

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Operator proposes a USD price update with any _contracts list
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Operator set does not reach either threshold (intentional non-participation)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: All future proposeUSDPrice calls revert due to ProposalInProgressError()
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Minimal if the attacker is the only active operator; otherwise requires exploiting operator inactivity

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: USD prices become stale forever; downstream reward/weighting logic can become inaccurate or halted
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium to high (depends on role rotation and whether role management is on-chain and responsive)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Any subsystem relying on usdPrice freshness

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Long-lived usdUpdateProposal.proposer != 0x0 with no subsequent updates
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Reward calculations drifting or disputes over stale pricing

validation_result.impact_summary: A USD price proposal that fails to reach either the approval or disapproval threshold becomes permanently stuck, blocking all future USD price updates indefinitely.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The LockManager contract implements a multi-sig USD price governance system with proposeUSDPrice, approveUSDPrice, and disapproveUSDPrice functions. The only two termination paths for a proposal are: (1) reaching APPROVE_THRESHOLD approvals, which triggers _execUSDPriceUpdate and deletes the proposal, or (2) reaching DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD disapprovals, which deletes the proposal. There is no time-based expiry, no admin cancel function, and no proposer cancel function. If neither threshold is reached (due to operator inactivity, key loss, disagreement, or deliberate griefing), the proposal persists forever. Since proposeUSDPrice reverts with ProposalInProgressError() when usdUpdateProposal.proposer != address(0), no new proposals can be created. Additionally, setUSDThresholds also reverts when a proposal is in progress, preventing the admin from lowering thresholds to unblock the situation. This is a genuine code defect — a missing lifecycle termination mechanism in the governance workflow.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code at LockManager.sol lines 142-143 confirms: `if (usdUpdateProposal.proposer != address(0)) revert ProposalInProgressError();` — this blocks new proposals when one exists. Lines 170-198 (approveUSDPrice) and lines 200-230 (disapproveUSDPrice) are the ONLY paths that can delete the proposal. Lines 126-132 (setUSDThresholds) also revert during an active proposal: `if (usdUpdateProposal.proposer != address(0)) revert ProposalInProgressError();`. A full search of the contract confirms no other function deletes or resets usdUpdateProposal.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: No expiry or cancellation mechanism exists. Once a proposal is created (proposer != address(0)), no new proposals can be submitted. The only exits are approval or disapproval thresholds, which may never be reached.
Admin cannot lower thresholds to unblock a stuck proposal because this function also reverts when a proposal is in progress. This eliminates the most obvious administrative recovery path.
One of only two termination paths. If insufficient operators vote to approve, the proposal remains.
Second of only two termination paths. If insufficient operators disapprove, the proposal remains. Combined with the approval path, if neither threshold is met, the proposal is permanently stuck.

calling_sequence: 1. PriceFeed operator calls proposeUSDPrice() — creates proposal, sets proposer != address(0), auto-approves (approvalsCount=1)
2. Remaining operators either don't vote, or vote in a split that doesn't reach either threshold
3. Any subsequent call to proposeUSDPrice() reverts at line 155: ProposalInProgressError()
4. Any call to setUSDThresholds() also reverts at line 129: ProposalInProgressError()
5. No other function in the contract can delete usdUpdateProposal
6. USD price updates are permanently blocked
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: proposeUSDPrice creates a proposal at line 155 that checks `usdUpdateProposal.proposer != address(0)`. The proposal can only be deleted in two places: (1) inside _execUSDPriceUpdate at line 437 (`delete usdUpdateProposal`) when approvalsCount >= APPROVE_THRESHOLD && disapprovalsCount < DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD, or (2) inside disapproveUSDPrice at line 225 (`delete usdUpdateProposal`) when disapprovalsCount >= DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD. With 5 PriceFeed roles and default thresholds of 3, if 1 operator proposes (auto-approves), and of the remaining 4, at most 1 approves and at most 2 disapprove (or operators are simply inactive), neither threshold is reached. The proposal becomes permanently stuck with no recovery mechanism.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts do not set USD thresholds or configure PriceFeed roles directly in the provided scripts. The default APPROVE_THRESHOLD=3 and DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD=3 are hardcoded in LockManager.sol.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: (1) Operator with PriceFeed_1 role calls proposeUSDPrice(1e18, [tokenA]) — proposal created with approvalsCount=1. (2) Operator with PriceFeed_2 approves (approvalsCount=2). (3) Operator with PriceFeed_3 disapproves (disapprovalsCount=1). (4) Operators with PriceFeed_4 and PriceFeed_5 are offline, have lost keys, or refuse to vote. (5) State: approvalsCount=2 < APPROVE_THRESHOLD(3), disapprovalsCount=1 < DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD(3). (6) All votes are cast; no further votes possible. (7) Proposal is permanently stuck. No new proposal can be created. No admin can intervene (setUSDThresholds also blocked). USD prices become stale forever. Even simpler: a single malicious operator proposes, and if the other 4 operators are all inactive, the proposal gets stuck with approvalsCount=1, disapprovalsCount=0.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH impact. USD price staleness affects getLockedWeightedValue() which is used by external contracts (likely AccountManager) for reward calculations. Stale prices cause incorrect weighted value calculations, potentially over- or under-rewarding players. The inability to update prices is permanent unless the admin can rotate PriceFeed roles to bring in new operators who haven't yet voted — but even this workaround relies on operational procedures not encoded in the contract. The code itself has no safety valve.
recommended_mitigations: Add a time-based expiry mechanism: if block.timestamp > usdUpdateProposal.proposedDate + PROPOSAL_EXPIRY_DURATION, allow the proposal to be deleted (either automatically in proposeUSDPrice or via a dedicated cancelExpiredProposal function)
Add an admin cancelProposal() function that can forcibly delete a stuck proposal
Allow the proposer to cancel their own proposal via a cancelUSDPrice() function
Remove the ProposalInProgressError check from setUSDThresholds so the admin can at least lower thresholds to unblock a stuck proposal
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LockManager with 5 PriceFeed accounts. 2. Have PriceFeed_1 call proposeUSDPrice(1e18, [token]). 3. Have PriceFeed_2 approve. 4. Have PriceFeed_3 disapprove. 5. Leave PriceFeed_4 and PriceFeed_5 idle. 6. Verify proposeUSDPrice reverts with ProposalInProgressError. 7. Verify setUSDThresholds also reverts with ProposalInProgressError. 8. Advance time by any amount and confirm no change. 9. Demonstrate permanent DoS on USD price governance.
uncertainties: Whether BaseBlastManager or inherited contracts provide any hidden mechanism to reset usdUpdateProposal (unlikely based on the architecture description but source not provided)
Whether the admin can rotate PriceFeed roles to bring in new addresses that haven't voted, providing an operational workaround (not a code fix)
Whether the configStorage system could be used to redeploy or replace the LockManager entirely as a recovery mechanism
additional_testing_needed: Verify no inherited function from BaseBlastManager can delete or modify usdUpdateProposal
Test the exact scenario where all 5 operators have voted (2 approve including proposer, 2 disapprove, 1 either way) and confirm the proposal is irrecoverable
Test role rotation: assign a PriceFeed role to a new address and verify the new address can vote on the existing proposal
